input: Audit this landing page copy for Slate, a B2B invoicing tool targeting freelance designers. Hero: 'Get paid faster.' Subheadline: 'Slate sends invoices, follows up automatically, and deposits to your bank.' CTA: 'Start free.' No social proof above the fold. Features section lists 9 items. Pricing is on a separate page. There is no FAQ. The only testimonial is from the founder's friend and says 'Really good product!'
rubric: identifies at least one TOFU/MOFU/BOFU stage mismatch (e.g., BOFU CTA with no MOFU trust-building)
finds 3 or more specific trust signal gaps (missing social proof types, vague claims, unverified stats)
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort with explicit reasoning
does not fabricate issues that are not described in the input
output is actionable — each finding maps to a concrete recommendation

input: Audit this copy block from the homepage of Vanta Supplements, a DTC protein powder brand. Above fold: large product photo, headline 'Clean protein. No compromise.' CTA: 'Shop now — $49.' No ingredient call-outs above fold. Below fold: ingredient list (dense, small font), 47 Amazon reviews quoted (no rating shown), and a 'Why Vanta?' section with 6 bullets (all feature-focused, none outcome-focused). No money-back guarantee mentioned anywhere.
rubric: identifies TOFU/MOFU/BOFU stage mismatches
finds 3+ trust signal gaps with specific evidence from the input
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort
does not invent issues absent from the described copy
distinguishes between quick wins (copy changes) and structural fixes (page layout)

input: Audit the following SaaS trial sign-up flow for Prism Analytics. Step 1: email + password form, no social login. Step 2: 7-field company profile form (company name, size, industry, role, use case, referral source, phone number — all required). Step 3: credit card required before trial starts. Step 4: empty dashboard with no onboarding prompt. Conversion from step 1 to step 4 is 6%.
rubric: identifies funnel stage mismatches (e.g., BOFU friction inserted at TOFU entry point)
identifies 3+ trust signal gaps or friction points with specific step references
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort — credit card gate vs. field reduction vs. empty state
no fabricated issues beyond what is described
output includes at least one hypothesis about why each gap hurts conversion

input: Audit this webinar registration page for Lumen Financial, an RIA targeting high-net-worth retirees. Headline: 'Join Our Free Webinar: Tax Strategies for 2025.' Date/time shown. Registration form: name, email, phone (required). No speaker bio. No agenda. No previous attendee count or testimonials. Legal disclaimer is 400 words, placed immediately below the form. Page has no exit-intent handling and no replay option mentioned.
rubric: identifies TOFU/MOFU/BOFU mismatches (e.g., high-friction form for cold traffic)
finds 3+ specific trust signal gaps given the high-trust audience (retirees, financial advice)
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort
does not fabricate issues
flags any compliance-adjacent trust signals that are missing for regulated industries

input: Audit the checkout page for Meridian Coffee, a DTC subscription brand. Products in cart: 2 x 12oz bag, $28 total. Page shows: item names, price, subtotal, shipping (calculated at next step), and a 'Place Order' button. No trust badges. No SSL indicator visible. No 'cancel anytime' messaging for the implied subscription. No upsell. No review snippet. Guest checkout available but buried below account creation form.
rubric: identifies TOFU/MOFU/BOFU stage issues (BOFU page missing MOFU reassurance)
finds 3+ trust signal gaps specific to checkout abandonment drivers
prioritizes by impact × effort with checkout-abandonment context
no fabricated issues
at least one finding addresses subscription-specific anxiety

input: Audit the 'Why Us' section of a B2B cybersecurity vendor (ShieldLayer) targeting enterprise IT buyers. Copy: 'We've been protecting businesses since 2009. Our team of experts uses cutting-edge technology to keep your data safe. Trusted by hundreds of companies worldwide.' No customer names. No case study links. No certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001) mentioned. No response-time SLA. No specific threat categories addressed.
rubric: identifies TOFU/MOFU/BOFU mismatches — enterprise buyers need MOFU proof at this stage
finds 3+ trust signal gaps with evidence from the copy
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort for enterprise sales cycles
does not fabricate specific claims or statistics
distinguishes between copywriting fixes and content/asset creation requirements

input: Audit this product page for Helio, a $299 portable EV charger. Hero image: product on white background. Headline: 'Charge anywhere.' Price shown. Specs: voltage, amperage, cable length, weight — no comparison to competitors. No compatibility checker. No reviews. No video. FAQs: 3 questions (all about shipping). Add-to-cart button visible. No financing option mentioned. Returns: '30-day returns, see policy' — policy is a link to a 1,200-word document.
rubric: identifies TOFU/MOFU/BOFU mismatches on the product page
finds 3+ trust signal and conversion gaps
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort for a $299 considered purchase
does not invent product details or issues not described
at least one finding addresses the considered-purchase decision process

input: Audit this onboarding email sequence for Flux, a project management SaaS. Email 1 (sent at signup): 'Welcome! Here are 12 things you can do in Flux.' Email 2 (day 2): 'Did you know about our integrations?' Email 3 (day 5): 'Upgrade to Pro — 50% off this week only.' Email 4 (day 7): 'We miss you. Come back.' No personalization tokens. No behavioral triggers. No single-action CTA per email. Unsubscribe rate after email 3: 18%.
rubric: identifies TOFU/MOFU/BOFU sequence mismatches (BOFU discount email before MOFU activation)
finds 3+ trust signal or engagement gaps in the sequence
prioritizes fixes by impact × effort, considering 18% churn signal
does not fabricate email content beyond what is described
at least one finding addresses behavioral trigger strategy vs. time-based triggers
